text.title: DR Drill Script Template Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the DR Drill Script Template methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Drill MUST start with an explicit declaration step: simulate the trigger condition + confirm the matching scenario id + announce to stakeholders + start the clock.
rule.rationale: Skipping declaration makes the drill timing invalid; clock-start anchors RTO measurement.

rule.statement: Runbook steps MUST be executed verbatim by the on-call engineer. Deviations are logged with rationale; ad-hoc fixes are flagged for post-mortem.
rule.rationale: Drilling the runbook surfaces gaps in the runbook; ad-hoc fixes mask the gap.

rule.statement: Drill is NOT green until restore validation passes: data integrity check + smoke test + transaction-replay sample. 'Service responds' alone is not validation.
rule.rationale: Cosmetic recovery (service up, empty DB) is the most common drill-as-theatre pattern.

rule.statement: Drill MUST capture RTO + RPO achieved with timestamps. Failure to hit targets is recorded; the drill is still useful but not 'passed'.
rule.rationale: Scoring lets the program track DR maturity over time; un-scored drills are just stories.

rule.statement: Post-mortem MUST be published within 7 days. Format: timeline + gaps + tickets + sign-off by engineering leader.
rule.rationale: Beyond 7 days, memory degrades + tickets don't get filed; the drill becomes lost data.

rule.statement: Drill announcement MUST go to all stakeholders impacted (security, support, business owners) at start + at restore + at post-mortem publish. Drill comms log captured.
rule.rationale: Silent drills produce real-incident panic in support; comms are part of the drill, not optional.
